finding: Human IgG1 kappa anti-PVRIG/CD112R monoclonal antibody
mechanistic_interpretation: This is an IgG1-format PVRIG blocker rather than a VHH; it complements COM701 IgG4 and VHH cases
modeling_or_docking_use: Use as IgG/Fab-level positive control and format-comparison case
caveat: Not an official SICBC positive sequence

finding: Complete heavy and light chain sequences available; sequence origin human; protein subtype IgG1 kappa
mechanistic_interpretation: Sequence-level public reference allows structure prediction of Fab/Fv
modeling_or_docking_use: Use VH/VL FASTA for Fv/Fab structure prediction and positive-control docking
caveat: Need ANARCI/IMGT re-numbering before CDR identity comparison

finding: SRF813 reportedly binds a distinct epitope on PVRIG
mechanistic_interpretation: Shows that blocker design should allow epitope diversity beyond COM701/Tab5/HR-151
modeling_or_docking_use: Use as anti-overfitting control: good poses need not mimic HR-151/Tab5
caveat: Public summary does not give residue-level epitope map

finding: SRF813 reportedly blocks interaction with CD112/PVRL2
caveat: No public complex structure or IC50 in easily accessible summary

finding: SRF813 enhances immune cell activation and shows preclinical in vivo antitumor activity
modeling_or_docking_use: Use as calibration for blocking-to-cell-function reasoning
caveat: Abstract-level evidence; exact assay data should be manually captured if needed

finding: Preclinical immune activation includes both NK and T cell activation
mechanistic_interpretation: Distinct epitope PVRIG blockade can activate both innate and adaptive arms
modeling_or_docking_use: Do not evaluate PVRIG only through T-cell readouts
caveat: Numerical data not fully public in press summary

finding: Binding to PVRIG inhibits PVRIG-PVRL2 interaction and may activate T lymphocytes and NK cells
mechanistic_interpretation: Independent mechanism statement consistent with checkpoint blockade
modeling_or_docking_use: Use as mechanism-reference source for report text
caveat: NCI mechanism wording is descriptive rather than residue-level

finding: Remzistotug may promote interaction between PVRL2 and CD226/DNAM-1
mechanistic_interpretation: Blocking PVRIG can redirect ligand availability toward activating CD226 axis
modeling_or_docking_use: Add CD226 axis as downstream mechanism label beyond steric blocking

molecule_or_source: GSK study register / NCI trial page
finding: Trial is listed as active-not-recruiting or closed-to-accrual depending registry page
mechanistic_interpretation: Clinical registry status is not the same as mechanism success
modeling_or_docking_use: Do not treat clinical status as docking validation
caveat: Status pages can lag or differ by registry

finding: GSK reportedly halted remaining CD226/PVRIG-axis phase 2 assets including GSK4381562
mechanistic_interpretation: Mechanism remains useful but should not be described as proven clinical success
caveat: Commercial status may change and should be rechecked
